name: Kat Abughazaleh
bio: Kat Abughazaleh is a Palestinian-American journalist and social media personality (formerly of Media Matters) running in the Democratic primary for Illinois's 9th congressional district. She has raised approximately $2.7M, largely through small-dollar online donations. (Source: FEC H6IL09178; Wikipedia)

jacket.note: ~$2.7M raised through Dec 31, 2025 per FEC filings. Average donation ~$32 — predominantly small-dollar. No corporate PAC money reported.

donors.name: Small-dollar individual donors — unitemized (grassroots, avg ~$32)
donors.amount_note: FEC confirmed: $2,247,721 in unitemized contributions — predominantly small-dollar grassroots

donors.name: Other committee/PAC contributions (minimal)
donors.amount_note: FEC confirmed: only $2,416 from other political committees — campaign explicitly rejects corporate PAC money

red_flags.label: Federal DOJ Indictment — ICE Facility Protest (Oct 2025)
red_flags.detail: Abughazaleh was federally indicted in October 2025 on charges of allegedly physically hindering and impeding a federal ICE agent during a protest at a Broadview, IL immigration detention facility in September 2025. She and supporters characterize the charges as political prosecution. Her case was pending as of the March 2026 primary.

red_flags.label: Federal indictment — impeding ICE agent (Oct 2025)
red_flags.detail: Abughazaleh was federally indicted in October 2025 for allegedly impeding an ICE agent at a Broadview, IL immigration detention facility protest in September 2025. Case pending as of March 2026. She has maintained the charges are politically motivated.

career_history.highlight: Built large progressive audience through short-form video

policy_platform.position: Opposes ICE detention facilities; advocates for humane immigration system. Federally indicted at Broadview ICE facility protest in Oct 2025.
Supports breaking up media monopolies; advocate for press freedom and anti-disinformation legislation.
Supports unconditional ceasefire and arms embargo on Israel. Palestinian-American identity central to campaign.

social_pulse.summary: Extremely high social media engagement for a first-time candidate. Small-dollar fundraising phenomenon. Federal indictment energized her base rather than suppressing it.

trust_indicators.label: No corporate PAC money — 100% small-dollar funded
Federal indictment pending (Oct 2025 ICE facility protest)
